bank: Reviews with frequent specific mentions of room features are truthful.
Reviews containing explicit temporal references (e.g., check-in times) are truthful.
Reviews with detailed descriptions of staff interactions are truthful.
Reviews using fewer first-person pronouns are deceptive.
Reviews with a high count of superlative adjectives (e.g., best, worst) are deceptive.
Reviews exhibiting consistently positive or negative sentiment throughout are deceptive.
Reviews showing mixed or balanced sentiment polarity are truthful.
Reviews containing multiple sensory descriptors (visual, auditory, olfactory) are truthful.
Reviews with abrupt topic shifts or inconsistent sentiment are deceptive.
Reviews using generic, vague language without concrete examples are deceptive.
Reviews with a higher density of named entities (places, people) are truthful.
Reviews that avoid personal experience terms (e.g., I saw, I heard) are deceptive.
Reviews with coherent narrative structure and logical flow are truthful.
Reviews exhibiting exaggerated emotional language are deceptive.
Reviews that reference external information rather than firsthand experience are deceptive.
